title: Centaurea nogmovii
intro: Centaurea nogmovii, also known as Balkan knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea nogmovii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into folk remedies, such as poultices and salves, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Centaurea nogmovii is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flower structure and leaf morphology, which are typical of the Asteraceae family. More specifically, Centaurea nogmovii is a member of the subfamily Carduoideae, which is a group of plants within the Asteraceae family known for their thistle-like flowers. The genus Centaurea, to which Centaurea nogmovii belongs, is a group of plants commonly known as knapweeds or centaureas. These plants are native to the Mediterranean region and are often found in dry, rocky areas.
